channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's team says 'no children' died from USAID cuts. Consider these 3 cases
item.description: Both Marco Rubio and Elon Musk, who led the effort to sunset the foreign aid agency, have said that no deaths have been linked to the cutting of its funding. These parents tell a different story.
item.content:encoded:
Both Marco Rubio and Elon Musk, who led the effort to sunset the foreign aid agency, have said that no deaths have been linked to the cutting of its funding. These parents tell a different story.

(Image credit: From left: David Augustine, Lameck Nyagudi and Kazeem Olawale Nasiru for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: What will the Pentagon begin screening 'war fighters' for? The quiz knows
item.description: Plus: Jurassic Park, U.K. politics, conspiracy theories, Pete Hegseth and numismatics.
item.content:encoded: Plus: Jurassic Park, U.K. politics, conspiracy theories, Pete Hegseth and numismatics.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Republican campaigns see immigration as a winning issue for midterms
item.description: Republicans are spending more money and running more ads on immigration than Democrats are ahead of the November midterm elections, according to an NPR analysis of advertisement data.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans are spending more money and running more ads on immigration than Democrats are ahead of the November midterm elections, according to an NPR analysis of advertisement data.

(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Kalshi says it's not a sportsbook even as World Cup bets surge
item.description: The betting site Kalshi emerged as a dominant sports betting platform during the World Cup. But the company avoids billions of dollars in taxes by insisting it is not a sports gambling operator.
item.content:encoded:
The betting site Kalshi emerged as a dominant sports betting platform during the World Cup. But the company avoids billions of dollars in taxes by insisting it is not a sports gambling operator.

(Image credit: Erin Hooley/AP Photo/Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Federal Reserve looks for 'secret sauce' behind a successful Wisconsin tool maker
item.description: Business is booming for Snap-on, a Wisconsin company that has made tools for professional mechanics for more than a century. It recently got a fact-finding visit from the head of the Chicago Federal Reserve Bank.
item.content:encoded:
Business is booming for Snap-on, a Wisconsin company that has made tools for professional mechanics for more than a century. It recently got a fact-finding visit from the head of the Chicago Federal Reserve Bank.

(Image credit: Scott Horsley)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: For one father and son, Alaskan blanket toss is a family tradition
item.description: A father and son discuss an old Alaskan whaling tradition known as the blanket toss.
item.content:encoded: A father and son discuss an old Alaskan whaling tradition known as the blanket toss.
item.dc:creator: Max Jungreis

item.title: At the World Cup, has 'VAR' gone too far? Some fans say it's ruining the tournament
item.description: In this World Cup, VAR, or video assistant referee, has become ubiquitous (and despised by many). But there was a time when fans and teams loved it. What went wrong?
item.content:encoded:
In this World Cup, VAR, or video assistant referee, has become ubiquitous (and despised by many). But there was a time when fans and teams loved it. What went wrong?

(Image credit: Alex Grimm)
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: China's Xi calls for step up of global effort in AI, as US curbs squeeze China's tech access
item.description: American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.
item.content:encoded:
American-led restrictions have blocked China from accessing some of the world's most advanced technologies, spurring China's efforts to build its own know-how and intensifying the tech race between the world's two biggest economies.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Rubio convenes countries on left-wing political violence
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday convened leaders from more than 60 countries to take part in the Trump administration's latest effort to quell what it calls "left wing" political terrorism.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Japan revises law on royals, keeps blocking women from throne
item.description: A popular princess drives support for having a female Japanese Emperor. But the country's first female prime minister opposes it.
item.content:encoded:
A popular princess drives support for having a female Japanese Emperor. But the country's first female prime minister opposes it.

(Image credit: Tomohiro Ohsumi)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn
